justification: Shaq blocked ALL five ACs due to AC5's ambiguity, producing zero implementation. AC1-AC4 are fully specified and unambiguous, yet no code was written. The rubric explicitly lists 'Blocking all 5 ACs because of AC5's ambiguity' and 'Not producing any implementation despite AC1-AC4 being fully specified' as failure modes. A passing score requires AC1-AC4 implemented with AC5 stubbed and escalated as a partial block.

observations.text: Escalation on AC5 is well-articulated with specific questions about the numbering format, responsibility, and generation mechanism
Correctly identified that AC5's 'company numbering policy' is undefined and cannot be guessed
Recognized AC1-AC4 as unambiguous in high_confidence_areas, yet contradictorily chose not to implement them
Full block instead of partial block — zero files changed, zero code produced despite 4 of 5 ACs being implementable
acceptance_criteria_coverage is empty — should show AC1-AC4 as implemented and AC5 as blocked
Shaq's own analysis acknowledges AC1-AC4 are 'unambiguous' and 'could be implemented immediately' but then doesn't implement them
Also flagged a secondary ambiguity on AC4 discount format (flat vs percentage) that wasn't warranted — this is overcautious scope creep on the escalation
